title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Shingles
intro: Herbal teas for Shingles are a natural and holistic approach to alleviate the symptoms of this painful viral infection. They work by reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and boosting the immune system, which is often weakened by the shingles virus. Examples of herbal teas that can help treat shingles include Ginger tea, which reduces pain and inflammation; Turmeric tea, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties; Chamomile tea, which soothes the nervous system and promotes relaxation; Peppermint tea, which cools the skin and reduces itching; Echinacea tea, which stimulates the immune system; and Lavender tea, which calms the mind and body. Additionally, other herbal teas such as Licorice root tea, Marshmallow root tea, and Wild yam tea may also be effective in treating shingles due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been used as a natural remedy to help alleviate the symptoms of shingles, a viral infection caused by the reactivation of the varicella-zoster virus. The anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce the severity and duration of shingles outbreaks by inhibiting the replication of the virus and reducing inflammation in the affected area. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea teas, including alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, contribute to its therapeutic effects by modulating the immune response and reducing oxidative stress. By incorporating Echinacea purpurea teas into their treatment regimen, individuals with shingles may experience reduced pain, inflammation, and discomfort, as well as a shorter recovery time.
preparation_procedure: Gather dried Echinacea purpurea flowers (about 1 tablespoon) and a cup of boiling water.
Place the Echinacea flowers in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Pour the boiling water over the Echinacea flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the Echinacea flowers.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally between meals, for relief from shingles symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to potentially alleviate shingles symptoms, but its use may be associated with side effects such as nausea, headaches, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat shingles, it is recommended to take precautions such as starting with small doses, avoiding excessive consumption, and monitoring for signs of adverse reactions, as well as being aware that it may interact with certain medications.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Echinacea purpurea teas for shingles.
Drink 1-2 cups of Echinacea purpurea tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried Echinacea purpurea per cup of boiling water.
Take Echinacea purpurea teas for 7-10 days to help alleviate symptoms.
Monitor your condition and adjust usage as directed by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat shingles, a viral infection causing painful rashes. The anti-inflammatory properties of Zingiber officinale teas help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with shingles, while its antiviral properties aid in combating the underlying virus. The bioactive constituents of Zingiber officinale teas, including gingerols and shogaols, have been found to possess potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral activities, which contribute to its effectiveness in treating shingles. Overall, the use of Zingiber officinale teas has been shown to provide relief from shingles symptoms, promote faster healing, and reduce the risk of complications associated with this ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Zingiber officinale root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, and stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help relieve shingles symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used as a complementary treatment for shingles, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions such as skin rashes or itching. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid taking Zingiber officinale teas if you have any underlying health conditions, such as bleeding disorders or low blood pressure, and to monitor your blood sugar levels if you have diabetes.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Zingiber officinale tea for shingles
Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day
Take 1 tablespoon of Zingiber officinale root powder with tea
Maintain dosage for 7-10 days or as directed by a doctor
Monitor shingles symptoms and adjust treatment as needed

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas have been traditionally used to help alleviate the symptoms of shingles, a viral infection caused by the reactivation of the varicella-zoster virus. The antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties of Melissa officinalis teas help to treat shingles by reducing pain, itching, and inflammation, making it easier to manage the condition. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and rosmarinic acid, contribute to its therapeutic effects by modulating the immune system and reducing oxidative stress. By using Melissa officinalis teas, individuals can benefit from reduced symptoms, improved quality of life, and a faster recovery time from shingles.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers. You can buy them at a health food store or online.
Use a cup of boiling water to make the tea. Pour the boiling water over the dried Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in a cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. This allows the herbs to release their active ingredients into the water.
Strain the tea into another cup to remove the dried leaves and flowers. Discard the solids.
Drink the tea 3-4 times a day to help manage shingles symptoms. You can also add honey or lemon to taste.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology", Melissa officinalis teas may be beneficial for shingles due to its lemon balm oil, which has been found to exhibit a direct antiviral effect on herpesviruses, including herpes simplex virus type 1 and 2, which cause shingles.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be used as a complementary treatment for shingles, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Melissa officinalis teas safely, take the recommended dosage, avoid consuming it if you are pregnant or breastfeeding, and be cautious when using it with other medications, especially blood thinners and sedatives.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Melissa officinalis tea for shingles treatment.
Drink 1 cup of Melissa officinalis tea 3 times a day for 7-10 days.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Melissa officinalis flowers per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons per cup if symptoms persist after 7 days.
Combine Melissa officinalis tea with other natural remedies for better results.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas, derived from the Cinchona tree, have been used to treat shingles due to their anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The quinine content in these teas helps to reduce the severity of shingles symptoms by inhibiting the replication of the herpes zoster virus, which causes the condition. The flavonoids and alkaloids present in Cinchona officinalis teas also exhibit immunomodulatory effects, which can aid in boosting the immune system's response to the virus. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat shingles include reduced pain, inflammation, and discomfort, as well as a shorter duration of the illness.
preparation_procedure: Get 2 tablespoons of dried Cinchona officinalis bark. You can buy it from a health food store or online.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot. Remove from heat.
Add the 2 tablespoons of Cinchona bark to the hot water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup. Discard the bark.
Drink the tea 3 times a day. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but not sugar.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat shingles due to their antiviral properties, but possible side effects may include nausea, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken to avoid overconsumption, which can lead to interactions with other medications and exacerbate conditions such as high blood pressure, and to monitor for signs of liver or kidney damage.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Cinchona officinalis tea 3 times a day.
Use 1/4 teaspoon of Cinchona bark per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes and strain the tea.
Drink the tea for 7-14 days to help manage shingles symptoms.
Consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat the shingles ailment, a condition caused by the reactivation of the varicella-zoster virus. The anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce the severity and duration of the symptoms associated with shingles, including pain, itching, and blistering. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, including flavonoids and terpenoids, have been shown to possess antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects that aid in the treatment of shingles. Drinking Ginkgo biloba teas may help to alleviate the symptoms of shingles, promote healing, and reduce the risk of postherpetic neuralgia, a common complication of the condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Place the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the tea infuser or strainer.
Steep the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Add honey or lemon to taste, then drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day to help alleviate shingles symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can potentially alleviate some symptoms of shingles, but they may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to their ability to thin blood and interact with certain medications. To minimize these risks, it's essential to take Ginkgo biloba teas in moderation, ideally under 240 mg per day, and avoid consuming them at least two weeks before any scheduled surgeries or medical procedures.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Ginkgo biloba for shingles treatment.
Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 3 times a day.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves per cup.
Steep for 5-10 minutes and drink without sugar or milk.
Continue treatment for 7-10 days or as advised by doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas have been traditionally used to treat the shingles ailment, which is caused by the reactivation of the varicella-zoster virus. The anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antiseptic properties of this herbal preparation help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the severity and duration of shingles symptoms. The bioactive constituents of Aloe barbadensis, including aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan, have been shown to exhibit antiviral and anti-inflammatory activities, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The benefits of using Aloe barbadensis teas to treat shingles include reduced pain and discomfort, accelerated healing, and reduced risk of complications, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Aloe barbadensis gel and 1 cup of boiling water.
Pour the boiling water into a container and add the Aloe gel. Stir well.
Let the mixture sit for 10-15 minutes to allow the Aloe to infuse into the water.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup. Discard the solids.
Drink the Aloe tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe shingles symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can be used to treat shingles, but may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation include avoiding consumption if pregnant or breastfeeding, monitoring blood sugar levels in diabetic patients, and being cautious of potential interactions with other medications, especially blood thinners and diabetes medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis tea 3 times a day.
Take 1 tablespoon of Aloe gel orally 3 times a day.
Soak a cloth in cooled tea and apply to affected area 2 times a day.
Repeat these steps for 7-10 days to see noticeable improvements.
Consult a doctor for ongoing treatment and advice on usage.

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort, have been traditionally used to treat the shingles ailment due to its antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antihistamine properties. The bioactive constituents of Hypericum perforatum, including hyperforin, hypericin, and flavonoids, help to reduce the severity and duration of shingles by inhibiting the replication of the varicella-zoster virus and reducing inflammation and pain. By using Hypericum perforatum teas, individuals may experience relief from shingles symptoms such as burning, itching, and numbness, as well as a reduction in the risk of postherpetic neuralgia. Overall, the benefits of using Hypericum perforatum teas to treat shingles include reduced pain and inflammation, accelerated healing, and a lower risk of long-term complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum flowers and 2 cups of water
Combine the flowers and water in a saucepan and bring to a boil
Reduce heat and let simmer for 5-7 minutes
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 3-4 times a day for shingles relief
preparations.preparation_study: According to BMC Veterinary Research, Hypericum perforatum teas may be beneficial in treating shingles due to its antibacterial and antifungal effects as well as its potential in wound healing and anti-inflammatory properties, which could aid in skin recovery.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can be used to treat shingles symptoms, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and diarrhea, as well as interactions with blood thinners and medications that affect blood sugar levels. To minimize risks, it is essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, avoid combining with other medications that may interact, and monitor blood sugar levels closely if you have diabetes.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Hypericum perforatum tea for shingles treatment.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Hypericum perforatum per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 3-4 cups of tea per day, 2-3 times a week, for 2-3 weeks.
Monitor your condition and adjust the dosage or frequency as advised by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat the shingles ailment, a painful condition caused by the reactivation of the varicella-zoster virus. The antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties of Valeriana officinalis teas help to soothe the skin, reduce pain and inflammation, and alleviate the associated nerve discomfort. The bioactive constituents of Valeriana officinalis teas, including valerenic acid, isovaleric acid, and valeranone, have been shown to possess antiviral and anti-inflammatory activities, which contribute to their therapeutic effects. By using Valeriana officinalis teas, individuals may experience relief from shingles symptoms, reduced pain and discomfort, and improved overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Valeriana officinalis roots or 2 tablespoons of dried root powder.
Combine the Valeriana officinalis with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the active ingredients to infuse.
Strain the tea into a separate cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the roots or powder.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day as needed, ideally before bedtime to help with sleep and pain relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to American Journal of Health-System Pharmacy: AJHP: official journal of the American Society of Health-System Pharmacists, Valeriana officinalis teas have been studied in formal trials for their potential efficacy in treating insomnia, but there is no specific information provided on their use for shingles.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can be used to treat shingles, but possible side effects include drowsiness, nausea, and stomach cramps. preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been traditionally used to treat the shingles ailment due to their anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, including flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, help to reduce the severity of shingles symptoms by inhibiting the replication of the herpes zoster virus and reducing inflammation. Drinking yarrow tea has been found to provide relief from the pain, itching, and rash associated with shingles, as well as help to promote healing and reduce the risk of complications. The benefits of using Achillea millefolium teas to treat shingles include reduced pain and discomfort, accelerated healing, and a lower risk of long-term nerve damage.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers.
Dry the leaves and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150°F (65°C) for 1 hour.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried Achillea millefolium in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to taste.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day to help alleviate shingles symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Medicines (Basel, Switzerland), Achillea millefolium teas, which are derived from the plant used by the Cherokee people, have potential biological activities that may support their traditional use in treating various diseases and ailments, but further study is needed to confirm their efficacy for shingles.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used to treat the shingles ailment, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation include using it in moderation, avoiding it if you have allergies or sensitivities, and being cautious if you have bleeding disorders or take blood thinners.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, derived from the licorice root, have been traditionally used to treat the shingles ailment due to their anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and immunomodulatory properties. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat shingles include reduced duration and severity of symptoms, improved quality of life, and potential reduction in the risk of postherpetic neuralgia.

Add honey to taste, if desired. Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day, 3-4 times a day.
Consult a doctor or a healthcare professional before using this tea for shingles treatment, especially if you have any underlying health conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Sambucus nigra teas have been traditionally used to treat the shingles ailment due to their anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help to alleviate the symptoms of shingles. The bioactive constituents present in Sambucus nigra teas, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been found to inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus, which is responsible for causing shingles. The antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties of Sambucus nigra teas help to reduce the severity and duration of shingles outbreaks, providing relief from the associated pain and discomfort. By consuming Sambucus nigra teas, individuals can benefit from the reduction of shingles symptoms, improved immune function, and accelerated healing process.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Sambucus nigra flowers or 1 tablespoon of dried flowers.
Combine the flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the flowers.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 3 times a day, sweetened with honey if desired.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Phytotherapy research, Sambucus nigra teas have potential immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects that could offer some protection and alleviation of shingles symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Sambucus nigra teas can be used to potentially alleviate the symptoms of shingles, but some people may experience side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its high content of glycosides. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase it, as well as to drink plenty of water and avoid consuming Sambucus nigra teas on an empty stomach.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle teas, have been traditionally used to treat the shingles ailment due to their anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, such as silymarin and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and combat the herpes zoster virus, thereby alleviating the symptoms of shingles. The benefits of this herbal preparation include reduced pain, itching, and blistering, as well as accelerated healing and improved quality of life for individuals affected by shingles. Regular consumption of Silybum marianum teas may also help to boost the immune system and prevent future outbreaks of the shingles virus.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Silybum marianum flowers and leaves.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried Silybum marianum in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day to help manage shingles symptoms.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to treat shingles, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners. To use Silybum marianum teas safely, one should be cautious of its high silymarin content, which can cause liver damage if taken in excessive amounts, and also be aware of the potential interactions with other herbal remedies.

preparations.herb_desc: Astragalus membranaceus teas have been traditionally used to treat the shingles ailment, which is caused by the reactivation of the varicella-zoster virus. The anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties of Astragalus membranaceus teas help to reduce the severity of shingles symptoms, including pain, itching, and rashes. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus membranaceus teas, such as polysaccharides, saponins, and flavonoids, have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory and antiviral activities that help to suppress the replication of the varicella-zoster virus and alleviate symptoms. Drinking Astragalus membranaceus teas may also benefit individuals with shingles by promoting relaxation, reducing stress, and enhancing immune function, ultimately leading to faster recovery and reduced risk of complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Astragalus membranaceus root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the Astragalus membranaceus root in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the root. Add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink 1-2 cups of Astragalus membranaceus tea 2-3 times a day for shingles relief.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Astragalus membranaceus teas can be used to treat the shingles ailment, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and diarrhea, as well as interactions with certain medications that may exacerbate the symptoms or worsen the condition. When using Astragalus membranaceus teas to treat shingles, it is recommended to start with small doses, monitor blood sugar levels closely, and avoid taking them during pregnancy or breastfeeding due to potential hormonal interactions and other potential complications.

preparations.herb_desc: Calendula officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat shingles, a painful and potentially debilitating viral infection caused by the reactivation of the varicella-zoster virus. The anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties of Calendula officinalis teas help to reduce the severity of shingles symptoms, such as pain, itching, and skin lesions. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids, have been shown to inhibit viral replication and promote wound healing, which helps to treat shingles effectively. Regular consumption of Calendula officinalis teas may also help to reduce the duration and severity of shingles outbreaks, providing relief from the associated pain and discomfort.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Calendula officinalis flowers.
Combine the dried flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and mix well.
Drink the tea 3 times a day, as needed, to help soothe shingles symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the provided study, there is no specific information about using Calendula officinalis teas for shingles, as the study primarily focuses on the wound healing properties of various plants.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Calendula officinalis teas can be used as a complementary treatment for shingles, although it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues in some individuals due to its potent antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. To use Calendula officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses, monitor your body's reaction, and avoid consuming it if you have sensitive skin or are prone to allergies, and also to be cautious when applying it topically to avoid skin irritation and infection.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas, derived from the rhizome of the turmeric plant, have been traditionally used to treat the shingles ailment, a painful and debilitating condition caused by the reactivation of the varicella-zoster virus. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation, particularly curcumin, a bioactive constituent, help to alleviate the symptoms of shingles by reducing nerve pain and inflammation. Curcuma longa teas also contain other bioactive constituents such as demethoxycurcumin and bisdemethoxycurcumin, which have been shown to possess antiviral and immunomodulatory effects, helping to reduce the severity and duration of shingles. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat shingles include reduced pain and discomfort, accelerated healing, and a lower risk of complications, making it a promising natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of Curcuma longa powder in a small saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat, then add the Curcuma longa powder. Stir until the powder dissolves completely.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup. Discard the solids.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help manage shingles symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Molecules (Basel, Switzerland), Curcuma longa teas, which are traditionally used as a complementary and alternative treatment ingredient, may be beneficial in managing shingles due to its anti-inflammatory, anti-microbial, antioxidant, and wound recovery properties.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be beneficial in treating shingles, but potential side effects may include mild stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions such as skin rashes or itching. Precautions when using Curcuma longa teas to treat shingles include taking it in moderation, avoiding excessive consumption, and being aware of interactions with other medications that may exacerbate symptoms or reduce its effectiveness.
